title: Fix handling of non-latin credentials in HTTPDigestAuth
body:
Fixes #6102. Update HTTPDigestAuth to handle non-latin credentials correctly.

- Change _basic_auth_str in src/requests/auth.py to encode username and password using utf-8 instead of latin1.
- Update build_digest_header in src/requests/auth.py to correctly format the string for the header with non-latin characters.
- Add tests in tests/test_requests.py to verify correct handling of non-latin credentials in HTTPDigestAuth.

step_plan.summary: Update HTTPDigestAuth to handle non-latin credentials by encoding credentials as UTF-8 and adjusting Digest header construction, plus add tests to validate non-latin handling.

steps.reason: To support non-latin credentials for Basic and Digest authentication by using UTF-8 encoding instead of latin1, ensuring correct handling of non-ASCII characters in username/password.
steps.side_effects: Potential changes to how the Authorization header is constructed in tests and downstream consumers; may require updates to tests that assert specific byte/string formats in the header; could affect any code that relied on latin1 behavior.

steps.operation: Adjust HTTPDigestAuth.build_digest_header to format the Digest header using utf-8 encoded values
steps.reason: Ensure the Digest header is correctly constructed when non-latin characters are present in credentials or related fields by aligning with UTF-8 encoding.
steps.side_effects: Digest computation/header formatting may change with UTF-8 inputs; tests depending on exact header formatting may need updates; verify compatibility with existing Digest/Proxy behaviors and downstream consumers.
